title: Hematoxylin and Eosin (H and E) Staining and Imaging
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5l99g","text":"This protocol details the H\u0026E staining and imaging.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"6d8mo","text":" ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"80dat","text":" Dako toluene-free mounting media (CS705)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4ij71","text":"Xenium HE Staining (CG000613 RevB, 10X Genomics)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"66ga","text":"NanoZoomer 2.0HT (Hamamatsu)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"id":962812,"guid":"37875C4A075611EF9C050A58A9FEAC02","name":"Sodium hydrosulfite","mol_weight":0,"sku":"157953","url":"https://www.sigmaaldrich.com/IN/en/search/157953-5g?focus=products\u0026page=1\u0026perpage=30\u0026sort=relevance\u0026term=157953-5G\u0026type=product","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":17,"name":"Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich)","link":"http://sigmaaldrich.com","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: For post-Xenium H&E staining, slides were quenched in 10 millimolar (mM) sodium hydrosulphite (Sigma Aldrich, 157953-5G) at Room temperature for 00:10:00 , rinse three times in water, then immediately processed through the following solutions; once in water for 00:02:00 , once in Hematoxylin (Sigma Aldrich, MHS16) for 00:20:00 , three times in water for 1 minute each, once in bluing solution (Dako, CS702) for 00:01:00 , once in water for 00:01:00 , once in 70% ethanol for 00:03:00 , once in 95% ethanol for 00:03:00 , once in eosin for 00:02:00 , twice in 95% ethanol for 30 seconds each, twice in 100% ethanol for 30 seconds each and twice in xylene for 3 minutes each as described in the Demonstrated Protocol Xenium HE Staining (CG000613 Rev B, 10X Genomics).
The slides were processed through water for 00:01:00 (1/3).
The slides were processed through water for 00:01:00 (2/3).
The slides were processed through water for 00:01:00 (3/3).
The slides were processed in 95% ethanol for 00:00:30 (1/2).
The slides were processed in 95% ethanol for 00:00:30 (2/2).
The slides were processed in 100% ethanol for 00:00:30 (1/2).
The slides were processed in 100% ethanol for 00:00:30 (2/2).
The slides were processed in xylene for 00:03:00 (1/2).
The slides were processed in xylene for 00:03:00 (2/2).
The slides were dried for 00:15:00 and then cover slipped using 1.5 mm thick cover glass and Dako toluene-free mounting media (CS705).

For post-CosMx H&E staining, the glass flow cell coverslip first removed by adhering clear sticky tape to the top of the flow cell, scoring around the inside of the adhesive edges of the flow cell, then peeling off the sticky tape leaving the adhesive edges attached to the slide and exposing the tissue for staining.

Slides were then washed by dipping into water several times to remove any glass shards. Next, the Demonstrated the Protocol Xenium HE Staining (CG000613 RevB, 10X Genomics) was followed as described in post-Xenium H&E staining.
Stained sections were covered using coverslips custom cut to fit inside the flow cell adhesive edges, using the glass scribe.
H&E stained sections were scanned on a NanoZoomer 2.0HT (Hamamatsu) using the 40X objective or a Zeiss AxioObserver 7 using a 20X objective (Zeiss).

All parameters were exact across three sites. These procedures were consistently applied across all sites: St. Jude Children's Research Hospital, Weill Cornell Medical Center, and the University of Adelaide.
